title: WeatherCAN - Canada.ca
content:
Get current weather, alerts, radar maps, and more with the WeatherCAN mobile app.
Stay informed wherever you are: Get current conditions and 7-day forecasts for more than 10,000 locations across Canada, including your current and saved locations.
Track air quality easily: Check your local Air Quality Health Index (AQHI) levels with just a tap.
Get weather alerts fast: Receive push notifications for severe weather, air quality updates, and extreme temperatures.
See the weather clearly: Use interactive radar features to zoom in on high-resolution radar animations, track precipitation types like rain, snow and hail by intensity, and view real-time lightning strikes across Canada. Learn more about interactive radar features .
Use it your way: Enjoy features that suit your preferences, such as dark mode, a home screen widget, and full support in English and French.
Learn something new: Discover weather facts and climate tips in the message centre. Note: message centre notifications cannot be turned off.

## Download WeatherCAN (free)
Click on the buttons below to visit the App Store for Apple users, or Google Play for Android to download the latest version of WeatherCAN.
Going out? Download our new weather app. Use your current location or add a new one.
Download our new weather app
Use your current location
See current conditions and the next 24 hours with detailed forecasts
Get quick access to our dynamic radar image
See the latest alerts and get notifications
Use our forecasts to plan ahead
Your weather wherever you are in Canada
Canada’s official weather source
Download on the App Store
Get it on Google play
Environment and Climate Change Canada’s (ECCC) WeatherCAN App (“the App”) makes ECCC forecasts and alerts available on mobile devices through real-time weather data, forecasts and push notifications (such as weather alerts for watches and warnings), making our weather information more accessible to Canadians.
Although ECCC meteorologists make efforts to update forecasts and alerts throughout the day, 7 days a week, weather conditions in your location may differ from information available from ECCC. All forecasts and warnings are produced with the aid of technology including:
- ground weather stations including weather buoys
- weather balloon data
- lightning detection network

### Acceptance of agreement
By using the App, you are agreeing to these terms of use (“Terms of Use”), as well as the Terms and conditions . These Terms of Use are a legal agreement between you and ECCC only and not Apple Inc. (Apple). The Terms of Use shall apply as long as you use the App and should be read carefully as they govern your use of the App and the relationship between you and ECCC. ECCC is solely responsible for the App and its content. You also acknowledge and agree that upon acceptance of these Terms of Use (through your use of the App), Apple and its subsidiaries and affiliates have the right to enforce them against you as third party beneficiaries. If you do not agree with ECCC’s Terms of Use, do not use this App.
You represent and warrant that you are of the legal age of majority in the jurisdiction of your residence, and that you have the right and authority to enter into this license agreement. If you are under the age of majority, your parent or guardian must agree to these Terms of Use for your download and use of this App.
The App and its contents are licensed on a “as is” and “as available basis”. To the maximum extent permitted by law, ECCC expressly disclaims all representations, warranties, obligations and conditions, whether express, implied or statutory, including implied warranties and implied conditions of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.
You acknowledge that alerts and forecasts provided by this App are provided to you as a resource for information only. While ECCC will make reasonable efforts to maintain up to date weather information on this App, you understand that the weather can change very quickly, and ECCC cannot guarantee that any information on its App, its servers or its affiliated websites and servers is accurate and up to date. In addition, the alerts and forecasts are generated based on the location information that you input into the App and if you input incorrect location information, or if your device or the App malfunctions, you may receive weather information that is not accurate for your location. You must exercise your judgement and carefully evaluate the content as ECCC will not be responsible for the consequences of any decisions or actions you take based on information provided in, or omitted from, any App content.
To the maximum extent permitted by law, ECCC expressly disclaims any liability for errors and omissions in the App’s content or for any losses that might result from your use of the App. In addition, neither ECCC nor Apple warrants that access to the App will be uninterrupted or error-free and neither will be responsible for your inability to use the App or for any malfunctions. To the maximum extent permitted by law, you understand and expressly agree that ECCC, its licensors, and Apple will not be liable to you under any theory of liability, whether based on breach of contract or tort (including negligence), product liability or otherwise. This disclaimer extends to any direct, indirect, incidental, special, consequential or exemplary damages that may be incurred by you, including loss of data, whether or not ECCC or its representatives have been advised or should have been aware of the possibility of those damages arising.
Apple shall not be responsible for any claims by you or any third party relating to your possession and/or use of this App, including but not limited to (i) product liability claims; (ii) any claim that this App fails to conform to any applicable legal or regulatory requirement or (iii) claims arising under consumer protection, privacy or similar legislation. In the event of a third party claim that this App or your possession and/or use of this App infringes that third party’s intellectual property rights, ECCC and not Apple, will be solely responsible for the investigation, defense, settlement and discharge of any such intellectual property infringement claim.
You hereby indemnify and hold harmless the Crown in Right of Canada, ECCC and their officers, employees, agents and contractors, from (and at ECCC’s request defend), any and all losses, liabilities, damages, costs or expenses (including reasonable attorney’s fees) arising out of any claim, action, or proceeding in any way connected to: (i) your use of the App; or your violation of (ii) these Terms of Use or (iii) any third party right.
You shall use this App strictly in accordance with these Terms of Use. You shall not (i) decompile, reverse engineer, disassemble, or attempt to derive the source code from the App; (ii) remove, alter or obscure any proprietary notice (including any notice of copyright or official mark) from any portion of the App; (iii) reproduce, modify, improve, enhance or make derivative works based upon the App (iv) distribute, license, lease, sell, resell, transfer, publicly display, transmit or otherwise exploit the App; (v) link to, mirror or frame any portion of the App; (vi) attempt to gain unauthorized access to, or impair any aspect of the App or its related systems or network; (vii) use the App for any illegal and unauthorized purpose, and agree to comply with all laws, rules and regulations applicable to your use of the App.
All personal information created, held or collected via this App by ECCC is protected under the Privacy Act . This means that you will be informed of the purpose for which it is being collected and how to exercise your right of access to that information. The “Current location” function on this App enables the App to provide weather information based on the current GPS location of your mobile device. In order to allow this functionality, data will be transmitted to ECCC for the purposes of identifying the mobile device geolocation information. If you do not wish to allow your GPS information to be transmitted to ECCC, please do not accept the request to use your mobile device geolocation when first launching the App. If you do accept or no longer want to use the geolocation function of the App, please disable the geolocation function in your device settings. If you choose to disable this function, the “Current location” will no longer work and you will have to enter all location information when using the App.
By using this App, you consent to the App collecting the following information from the device you are using: the type of device you are using to access the App, information for use in the App, and your preferred language. This data is collected and used in the provision of weather data for your mobile device’s current location. The geolocation function in the App uses the GPS mapping function in your device to determine the location of your device and compare it against weather forecast regions. If you do not want to allow your GPS information to be transmitted to the App, please turn this feature off in your device settings.
If you choose to use the feedback form in the App, the following information is collected: device model, version of App in use and your email address, along with any information you choose to provide in the text input field. The information that you provide is collected in order to respond to your questions and comments. It is also protected under the Privacy Act .
Questions or comments regarding this privacy notice or the administration of the Privacy Act at ECCC may be directed to:
Director Access to Information and Privacy Telephone: 819-938-3580 Email: ECATIP-ECAIPRP@ec.gc.ca
If you are not satisfied that we have adequately respected your privacy, you may wish to contact the Office of the Privacy Commissioner by calling their information center at 1-800-282-1376 or by visiting their contact page .

### Modifications and access
ECCC reserves the right to modify, suspend your access to or discontinue the App and terminate these Terms of Use for any reason and at any time and without any notice or liability to you.
ECCC reserves the right to modify these Terms of Use in its sole discretion. ECCC will make reasonable efforts to notify you of such modifications. For example, ECCC may generate a pop-up or similar notifications (such as weather alerts for watches and warnings and App updates) when you access the App for the first time after such material changes are made. Your continued use of the App following such modifications constitutes your agreement to the new terms. ECCC recommends that you review the Terms of Use from time to time.
ECCC grants you a revocable, non-transferable, non-exclusive and limited license to install and use the App for your personal, non-commercial purposes. You may use this App on an iPhone, iPod Touch, iPad, or other Apple device that you own or control, and as permitted by the Apple Store Terms of Service (Usage Rules) and its Family Sharing program.
You may copy the App’s content for your own personal use only. You may not alter the App or use it for commercial purposes. If you are interested in weather data for any other purpose, consult the Government of Canada’s open data portal .
The App and all rights therein are and shall remain ECCC’s property or the property of ECCC’s licensors. Neither these Terms of Use nor your use of the App convey to you any rights: (i) in or related to the App except for the limited license granted above or ii) to use or reference in any manner ECCC or its logos, product and service names, trademarks or service marks or those of ECCC’s licensors.

### Maintenance and support
You and ECCC each acknowledge and agree that Apple will not provide any maintenance or support for the App. ECCC may provide periodic updates to the App which may include bug fixes and enhancements.
These Terms of Use are governed by the laws in force in the province of Ontario and by the federal laws of Canada, as applicable. You agree that all actions or proceedings arising directly or indirectly out of these Terms of Use or your use of the App shall be litigated in the courts of Ontario or the Federal Court of Canada, located in Ontario, Canada.

### U.S. legal compliance
You represent and warrant that (i) you are not located in a country that is subject to a U.S. Government embargo, or that has been designated as a “terrorist supporting country” and ii) you are not listed on any U.S. Government list of prohibited or restricted parties.

### Third party terms of agreement
The App has the functionality to launch and/or interact with third party applications on your mobile device. Your use of such third party applications is subject to and must be in compliance with the respective terms and conditions of those applications.
Should you have any questions, claims or functionality issues related to this App, please contact us.

## Set up custom air quality notifications
Set up your own custom notifications for air quality in one or multiple locations.
- Open the WeatherCAN app.
- Tap on the ‘More’ tab in the lower right-hand corner of the display.
- Tap “Notifications”.
- Tap “Air Quality”. AQHI information is only available for locations where data can be collected. If your location does not have air quality data, you can set notifications for a nearby area. You can also contact your local public health office, Ministry of Environment, or local lung and asthma association chapter for existing local information on air quality and your health.
- AQHI information is only available for locations where data can be collected.
- If your location does not have air quality data, you can set notifications for a nearby area.
- You can also contact your local public health office, Ministry of Environment, or local lung and asthma association chapter for existing local information on air quality and your health.
- Select “Enable AQHI Notifications”.
- To pick the same AQHI level at all saved locations: choose “Same Level for All Locations” and select your desired level.
- To pick different AQHI levels at different locations: tap on each location name separately and choose the level you want.
WeatherCAN: Canada’s official weather source
Now with air quality health index notifications you can customize based on your sensitivity to air pollution
To protect yourself and those you care about from the effects of air pollution.
WeatherCAN: Canada’s official weather source

### What is the Air Quality Health Index?
The Air Quality Health Index or "AQHI" is a scale designed to help you understand what the air quality around you means to your health. The AQHI is measured on a scale ranging from 1-10+. Learn more about the Air Quality Health Index .

### When do AQHI notifications happen?
Once you have set up your notifications for a specific location and level of air quality, you will be notified when the air quality, compared to the previous hour:
- reaches your chosen AQHI level or higher
- goes below your chosen AQHI level after reaching it earlier

### Choosing the right AQHI level for alerts
- You should choose your AQHI level using your own experience and symptoms as a guide
- If you are sensitive to air pollution , you may want to set notifications for the moderate risk category (4 to 6)
- If you are not sensitive to pollution, you may only want to set notifications for when AQHI reaches the high-risk category (7 to 10)

### How often do you get AQHI notifications?
AQHI conditions are updated every hour. The number of AQHI notifications you get will depend on:
- how many locations you choose
- the AQHI level you choose for each location

### How can I limit the number of alerts?
- turn off notifications for more than one location
- increase your chosen threshold of AQHI

### Custom AQHI notifications are not air quality advisories
You can set your AQHI notifications for any value between 3 and 10+.
However, Special Air Quality Advisories only go out when there is a high level of health risk to the general population (when the AQHI reaches 7).
As a result, if you or someone in your care is sensitive to air pollution , it can be helpful to set AQHI alerts for lower levels, and to take precautions before the AQHI is too high.

### Related air quality links
- Air Quality Health Index
- Air Quality Health Index: local conditions

## Set up custom temperature notifications
Set up your own custom notifications for high and low temperatures, humidex and wind chill. You can set notifications for multiple locations at the same thresholds.
These custom notifications alert you when the observed temperature reaches, or the forecast temperature is expected to reach, your chosen value(s) so you can make informed decisions using up to date data.
- Open the WeatherCAN app.
- Tap on the ‘More’ tab in the lower right-hand corner of the display.
- Tap “Notifications”.
- Set the “Temperature Notifications” to On.
- Set the temperature, humidex, and/or wind chill from -60°C to 45°C by scrolling through the values and tapping on the desired value(s). These values will be applicable for all locations that you have turned on temperature notifications. Individual thresholds for each location cannot be set due to technical limitations.
- Select “All Locations” to receive notifications for your current location and all saved locations. Alternatively, select individual locations by setting them to On if you only want to receive notifications for certain sites.

## How to leave feedback
You can leave feedback directly in the app. Tap the "More" button in the bottom navigation, go to the "About us" section and select "Feedback".
If you're not able to leave feedback in the app, use the Contact us page . Select the Problem or feedback button, then choose WeatherCAN app from the dropdown menu.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
